metadata.title: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men
metadata.study_design: Intravenous infusion of [U-13C]palmitate during prolonged overnight fast (12-19h) in healthy, lean young men and women
metadata.demographics: Healthy, lean young men and women (n=12), matched for age and BMI, free from disease, weight stable, not taking lipid-lowering medication

evidence.quote: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men
British JournalofNutrition(2011),105,857–865
Kyriakoula Marinou1,2, Martin Adiels3, Leanne Hodson1, Keith N. Frayn1, Fredrik Karpe1 and Barbara A. Fielding1*
Volunteers were studied during a prolonged overnight fast (12–19h) using an intravenous infusion of [U-13C]palmitate
Healthy,leanyoungmenandwomen(n12)werestudied,andmatchedforageandBMIwithnosignificantdifference in waist circumference
Healthy, lean young men and women (n 12) were studied

methods_participants.label: Inclusion criteria - health status
methods_participants.value: Healthy, lean, free from any disease, weight stable

evidence.quote: Subjects, recruited from the wider Oxford community via advertisement, were free from any disease, weight stable and not taking any lipid-lowering medication or medication that would alter lipid metabolism
All individuals were selected to have a BMI of less than 25 kg/m²

methods_participants.label: Study timing - menstrual cycle phase
methods_participants.value: Follicular phase (first week of menstrual cycle)

evidence.quote: The studies were performed during the first week (follicular phase) of the menstrual cycle

methods_participants.label: Pre-study meal
methods_participants.value: Low-fat meal on evening before study

evidence.quote: Before the study day, subjects were asked to avoid foodstuffs naturally enriched in 13C for 48h
Fat mass was measured by bioelectrical impedance analysis, and fat-free mass was calculated as the difference between total body weight and fat mass

methods_participants.label: Assay: fatty acid analysis
methods_participants.value: GC and GC-MS for isotopic enrichment

evidence.quote: A cannula was inserted into an antecubital vein, and a baseline blood sample was taken for background isotopic enrichment
At time 0, a continuous intravenous infusion (0·04 mmol/kg per min) of potassium [U-13C]palmitate

methods_participants.label: 3-OHB isotopic enrichment measurement
methods_participants.value: NEFA, VLDL-TAG and 3-OHB concentrations determined enzymatically

evidence.quote: plasma NEFA, VLDL-TAG and 3-OHB concentrations were determined enzymatically

methods_participants.label: VLDL isolation - centrifugation speed
methods_participants.value: Sequential flotation using density gradient ultracentrifugation

evidence.quote: In ten subjects, VLDL (Svedberg flotation rate (Sf) 60–400) and VLDL (Sf 20–60) were isolated by sequential flotation

methods_participants.label: Inclusion criteria - BMI matching
methods_participants.value: Matched for age and BMI with no significant difference in waist circumference

evidence.quote: matched for age and BMI with no significant difference in waist circumference
This was continued until the end of the experiment (at 7h).

methods_participants.label: Ethics approval
methods_participants.value: Oxford Shire Clinical Research Ethics Committee

evidence.quote: All procedures involving human subjects were approved by the Oxfordshire Clinical Research Ethics Committee

methods_participants.label: Informed consent
methods_participants.value: Written informed consent obtained from all subjects

evidence.quote: Written informed consent was obtained from all subjects

methods_participants.label: Assay: 3-OHB isotopic enrichment
methods_participants.value: Modified method of Beylot et al.

evidence.quote: Isotopic enrichment from [U-13C]fatty acids appearing in 3-OHB in deproteinised plasma was measured using a modified method of Beylot et al.

narrative_results.statement: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men

evidence.quote: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men

narrative_results.statement: After 12h fasting, women had a more advantageous metabolic profile with lower plasma glucose and TAG but higher plasma NEFA concentrations

evidence.quote: After 12h fasting, the women had a more advantageous metabolic profile with lower plasma glucose (P,0·05) and TAG (P,0·05) but higher plasma NEFA (P,0·05) concentrations

narrative_results.statement: In women, systemic plasma fatty acids were the major source of 3-OHB production, but this was not observed in men

evidence.quote: In women, the high isotopic enrichment of plasma 3-OHB suggested that systemic plasma fatty acids were the major source of 3-OHB production. However, in men, this was not observed during the course of the study (P,0·01)

narrative_results.statement: There were no sex differences for the incorporation of 13C into VLDL-TAG

evidence.quote: There were no sex differences for the incorporation of 13C into VLDL - or VLDL -TAG

discrepancies.pass_2_value: Pass_1 includes both strenuous exercise AND alcohol avoidance for 24 hours; pass_2 only mentions exercise avoidance, omitting alcohol restriction
Pass_1 describes baseline blood sampling procedure; pass_2 describes fatty acid analysis method. These are different aspects of the study with no overlap

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: The ability of young women to partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG may contribute to their more advantageous metabolic profile compared with young men.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: After 12h fasting, the women had a more advantageous metabolic profile with lower plasma glucose (P,0·05) and TAG (P,0·05) but higher plasma NEFA (P,0·05) concentrations
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: After 12h fasting, the women had a more advantageous metabolic profile with lower plasma glucose (P<0·05) and TAG (P<0·05) but higher plasma NEFA (P<0·05) concentrations.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: the transfer of 13C from [U-13C]palmitate to plasma [13C]3-OHB reached a plateau 6–7 h after the start of the infusion in women but was still increasing at 6 h in men
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: the transfer of 13C from [U-13C]palmitate to plasma [13C]3-OHB reached a plateau 6–7h after the start of the infusion in women but was still increasing at 6h in men.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: The transfer of 13C from palmitate to plasma 3-OHB reached a plateau 6-7 hours after infusion start in women but was still increasing at 6h in men
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Transfer of 13C from palmitate to plasma 3-OHB reached a plateau 6-7h after infusion start in women but was still increasing at 6h in men

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: In women, the high isotopic enrichment of plasma 3-OHB suggested that systemic plasma fatty acids were the major source of 3-OHB production. However, in men, this was not observed during the course of the study (P,0·01)
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: In women, the high isotopic enrichment of plasma 3-OHB suggested that systemic plasma fatty acids were the major source of 3-OHB production. However, in men, this was not observed during the course of the study (P<0·01).

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: There were no sex differences for the incorporation of 13C into VLDL - or VLDL -TAG
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: There were no sex differences for the incorporation of 13C into VLDL - or VLDL -TAG.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: The ratio of [13C]3-OHB:[13C]VLDL-TAG in the plasma was significantly higher in women than in men (2·73 (SE 1·7) v. 0·66 (SE 0·30); P¼0·027 for VLDL1 and 3·24 (SE 1·6) v. 0·97 (SE 0·43); P¼0·014 for VLDL2)
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: The ratio of [13C]3-OHB:[13C]VLDL-TAG in the plasma was significantly higher in women than in men (2·73 (SE 1·7) v. 0·66 (SE 0·30); P¼0·027 for VLDL1 and 3·24 (SE 1·6) v. 0·97 (SE 0·43); P¼0·014 for VLDL2).

semantic_groups.rationale: Identical sex distribution reported in both extractions
Same age value for men; pass_1 includes 'Participant' prefix while pass_2 omits it
Same age value for women; pass_1 includes 'Participant' prefix while pass_2 omits it
Identical inclusion criteria for health status reported in both extractions
BMI inclusion criterion present in pass_1 but absent in pass_2
Same menstrual cycle phase information; minor wording differences in phrasing order
Same 48-hour avoidance period and 13C-enriched foodstuff restriction; different phrasing
Pass_1 includes both strenuous exercise AND alcohol avoidance for 24 hours; pass_2 only mentions exercise avoidance, omitting alcohol restriction
Both describe low-fat meal; pass_2 adds temporal detail (evening before study) that is complementary, not contradictory
Both describe overnight fasting; pass_1 includes 'Study' qualifier which is negligible difference
Both describe bioelectrical impedance analysis for body composition; pass_1 includes fat mass/fat-free mass details that complement pass_2
Pass_1 describes baseline blood sampling procedure; pass_2 describes fatty acid analysis method. These are different aspects of the study with no overlap
Both describe isotope tracer infusion with same dose (0.04 mmol/kg per min), same compound (potassium [U-13C]palmitate), and same method (continuous IV infusion); minor wording variations
Both state isotope tracer purity is 98%; only formatting difference
Both specify 7 hours infusion duration; pass_1 includes additional context about continuous infusion until end of experiment
Both describe enzymatic determination/assay of plasma NEFA; minor wording variations only
Both describe plasma insulin measurement using radioimmunoassay (RIA). Only minor reordering of terms.
Both describe VLDL isolation by sequential flotation using density gradient ultracentrifugation. Pass 1 includes sample size (N=10) which is additional context, not conflicting.
Both specify 40000 rpm centrifugation speed for VLDL isolation. Pass 1 includes additional rotor detail (SW40Ti swinging bucket rotor) which is complementary.
Pass 1 contains VLDL isolation temperature (15°C) with no corresponding entry in Pass 2.
Pass 1 contains fatty acid analysis method (GC and GC-MS) with no corresponding entry in Pass 2.
Pass 1 contains 3-OHB isotopic enrichment measurement method with no corresponding entry in Pass 2.
Both extractions identically report 12 participants.
Pass 2 contains inclusion criteria regarding BMI matching with no corresponding entry in Pass 1.
Pass 1 is empty; Pass 2 contains ethics approval information. No conflict, but information only exists in one extraction.
Pass 1 is empty; Pass 2 contains informed consent information. No conflict, but information only exists in one extraction.
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
Same fact with negligible wording differences: 'The transfer of' vs 'Transfer of' and '6-7 hours' vs '6-7h'
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
